rankings.title: Missing Men, Rising Women — WWII Selective Mortality and the Individual Origins of Gender Convergence
conditions: a defensible instrument/first stage not mechanically tied to local labor-demand trends
explicit pre-trend diagnostics using 1930–40
a design that separates mobilization from war-production intensity/migration shocks

rankings.title: The Selective Survivor — Mortality Selection, the Missing Fifth, and What Aggregate Gender Gaps Actually Measure
conditions: an explicit linkage-selection framework—IPW/bounds/validation—
a clear claim that materially changes interpretation of existing WWII gender-convergence evidence

rankings.title: The Marriage Market After Armageddon — Sex Ratio Shocks and Assortative Matching
The Great Reshuffling — WWII, Internal Migration, and the Geographic Origins of Gender Convergence
conditions: a highly defensible exogenous shock measure—e.g.
plausibly predetermined contract allocation components—
a design that addresses mover selection head-on

rankings.title: Born Under Rosie — Maternal Wartime Employment and the Intergenerational Transmission of Gender Norms
Idea 1: Missing Men, Rising Women — WWII Selective Mortality and the Individual Origins of Gender Convergence
conditions: abandoning the agricultural IV
finding a credible source of exogenous variation for mobilization
such as local draft board idiosyncrasies
or relying on a tightly controlled event-study

rankings.title: Idea 4: The Great Reshuffling — WWII, Internal Migration, and the Geographic Origins of Gender Convergence
conditions: developing a credible instrument for defense contract allocation
such as physical geography constraints or a shift-share design

rankings.title: Idea 5: The Selective Survivor — Mortality Selection, the Missing Fifth, and What Aggregate Gender Gaps Actually Measure
Idea 3: The Marriage Market After Armageddon — Sex Ratio Shocks and Assortative Matching
Idea 2: Born Under Rosie — Maternal Wartime Employment and the Intergenerational Transmission of Gender Norms
Missing Men, Rising Women — WWII Selective Mortality and the Individual Origins of Gender Convergence
conditions: confirming tight Lee bounds
pre-trends visuals in 1930-1940
integrating Idea 5 decomposition as core chapter

rankings.title: The Selective Survivor — Mortality Selection, the Missing Fifth, and What Aggregate Gender Gaps Actually Measure
conditions: bundling as lead chapter in Idea 1 project
sensitivity to link quality
